name: Hard-coded Password
description: A plaintext password is embedded directly in the IaC script rather than being sourced from a secrets manager or environment variable.
fix_example: Replace hardcoded password with a reference to a secrets manager variable. In Ansible: use vault_password_file or Vault-encrypted variables. In Terraform: use var.password with sensitive = true. In Kubernetes: use a Secret resource instead of plaintext env vars.

name: Hard-coded Credential
description: An API key, access key, secret key, or token is embedded directly in the IaC script.
fix_example: Use environment variables (e.g., AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID) or IAM instance profiles instead of hardcoded credentials. In Terraform: remove credentials from provider blocks and rely on the AWS credential chain.

name: Overly Permissive CIDR
description: A security group or firewall rule allows unrestricted inbound or outbound access from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0, exposing services to the entire internet.
fix_example: Restrict CIDR blocks to known IP ranges. Replace 0.0.0.0/0 with specific CIDR ranges. For Terraform security groups: set cidr_blocks = ["10.0.0.0/8"] or use security group references instead of CIDR ranges.
references: Rahman et al. 2019 (Seven Sins - Unrestricted IP Binding)

name: Privileged Container
description: A container is configured with privileged: true, granting it full access to the host system including all capabilities and devices.
fix_example: Remove privileged: true from securityContext. Use specific capabilities with securityContext.capabilities.add instead of full privilege. Add allowPrivilegeEscalation: false.

name: Run as Root
description: A container or process is configured to run as the root user (UID 0), violating the principle of least privilege.
fix_example: Add USER instruction in Dockerfile with a non-root user (e.g., USER 1000). In Kubernetes: set runAsUser to a non-zero UID and runAsNonRoot: true in securityContext. In Ansible: avoid become_user: root and use specific users.
references: Rahman et al. 2019 (Seven Sins - Admin by Default)

name: Missing Encryption at Rest
description: Storage resources (EBS volumes, RDS instances, S3 buckets) are configured without server-side encryption, exposing data at rest.
fix_example: For EBS: set encrypted = true in aws_ebs_volume or aws_instance root_block_device. For RDS: set storage_encrypted = true. For S3: add aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration with AES256 or aws:kms algorithm.
references: Rahman et al. 2019 (Seven Sins - Missing Integrity Check)

name: TLS Certificate Validation Disabled
description: TLS or SSL certificate validation is explicitly disabled, allowing man-in-the-middle attacks.
fix_example: Remove or set validate_certs: yes in Ansible get_url, uri, and other network modules. Never set ssl_verify: false. Configure proper CA certificates instead of disabling validation.
references: Rahman et al. 2019 (Seven Sins - HTTP without TLS)

name: World-Writable File Permissions
description: Files or directories are configured with overly permissive permissions (mode 0777 or 0666), allowing any user to read and write.
fix_example: Use restrictive file permissions: mode: '0640' for configuration files, mode: '0600' for sensitive files. Avoid mode: '0777'. Use owner and group parameters to restrict access.

name: Passwordless Sudo
description: A user or service account is granted NOPASSWD sudo privileges, allowing privilege escalation without authentication.
fix_example: Remove NOPASSWD from sudoers configuration. Require password for sudo. Use specific command allowances instead of ALL. Consider using role-based access control.
references: Rahman et al. 2019 (Seven Sins - Admin by Default)

name: SSH Root Login Enabled
description: SSH is configured to allow direct root login (PermitRootLogin yes), which enables brute-force attacks targeting the most privileged account.
fix_example: Set PermitRootLogin no in sshd_config. Use a non-root admin account with sudo for privileged operations. Consider setting PermitRootLogin prohibit-password if key-based access is required.

name: World-Readable Private Key
description: A private key file is deployed with overly permissive read permissions (e.g., mode 0644), allowing any system user to read the private key.
fix_example: Set mode: '0600' or mode: '0400' for private key files. Set owner to the service account that needs the key. Ensure group and other permissions are revoked.

name: No Resource Limits
description: Container resource limits (CPU, memory) are not defined, creating a risk of resource exhaustion or denial-of-service due to runaway processes.
fix_example: Add resources.limits and resources.requests to container specs in Kubernetes. Set memory: "512Mi" and cpu: "500m" as limits. Set requests below limits for proper scheduling.

name: Hardcoded Secret in Environment Variable
description: Sensitive values (passwords, API keys, tokens) are passed as plaintext environment variables in container or deployment configurations.
fix_example: Use Kubernetes Secrets instead of hardcoded env values. Reference secrets with valueFrom.secretKeyRef. In Docker: use Docker secrets or environment variable substitution at runtime, never bake secrets into the image.

name: Host Network Access
description: A Kubernetes pod uses hostNetwork: true, sharing the host node's network namespace and bypassing network isolation.
fix_example: Remove hostNetwork: true from pod spec. Use ClusterIP or NodePort services for inter-pod communication. Only use hostNetwork for system-level pods that genuinely require host network access.

name: Writable Root Filesystem
description: Container root filesystem is writable (readOnlyRootFilesystem: false), allowing an attacker who gains code execution to modify system files.
fix_example: Set readOnlyRootFilesystem: true in securityContext. Mount writable volumes only where needed (e.g., /tmp, /var/log). This limits an attacker's ability to persist changes after container compromise.

name: Secret in ConfigMap
description: Sensitive information (passwords, tokens, certificates) is stored in a Kubernetes ConfigMap instead of a Secret resource.
fix_example: Move sensitive data from ConfigMaps to Kubernetes Secrets. Reference secrets with envFrom.secretRef or volumes.secret. Consider using external secrets operators (e.g., External Secrets Operator) for production.

name: Automount Service Account Token
description: automountServiceAccountToken is set to true, automatically mounting an API token into the container that can be used to access the Kubernetes API.
fix_example: Set automountServiceAccountToken: false on pods that do not need API access. Only mount service account tokens explicitly where required. Use RBAC to restrict the permissions of service accounts.

name: Unpinned Base Image
description: A Dockerfile uses a mutable image tag (e.g., :latest) instead of a pinned digest or specific version tag, creating non-reproducible builds and supply chain risk.
fix_example: Pin base images to specific digests: FROM ubuntu@sha256:... or to immutable version tags: FROM ubuntu:22.04. Avoid :latest tags. Use Dependabot or Renovate to keep pinned versions up to date.

name: Broad COPY Instruction
description: A Dockerfile uses COPY . . which copies the entire build context including .git directories, .env files, and other sensitive files into the image.
fix_example: Replace COPY . . with specific COPY instructions for required files. Create a .dockerignore file to exclude .git, .env, *.key, *.pem, node_modules, and other sensitive or unnecessary files.

name: Missing HEALTHCHECK
description: A Dockerfile does not include a HEALTHCHECK instruction, making it impossible for container orchestrators to detect and recover from unhealthy containers.
fix_example: Add HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --retries=3 CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1. This enables Docker and Kubernetes to monitor container health.

name: Publicly Accessible Database
description: A database instance (RDS, PostgreSQL, MySQL) is configured with publicly_accessible = true, exposing it to the internet.
fix_example: Set publicly_accessible = false on RDS instances. Place databases in private subnets. Use VPC security groups to restrict access to specific CIDR ranges or security group IDs.

name: Backup Disabled
description: Automated backups are disabled for a stateful service (e.g., backup_retention_period = 0 for RDS), creating a risk of unrecoverable data loss.
fix_example: Set backup_retention_period >= 7 on RDS instances. Enable automated backups and cross-region backup copies for critical databases. Test restore procedures regularly.

name: IMDSv2 Not Required
description: The EC2 Instance Metadata Service is configured with http_tokens = optional instead of required, allowing IMDSv1 requests without a session token.
fix_example: Set http_tokens = required in the metadata_options block of aws_instance. This enforces IMDSv2 which requires a session-oriented token, preventing SSRF attacks from accessing instance metadata.

name: S3 Public Access Block Disabled
description: One or more S3 public access block settings are false, potentially allowing public bucket policies or ACLs to expose data.
fix_example: Set all four public access block settings to true: block_public_acls = true, block_public_policy = true, ignore_public_acls = true, restrict_public_buckets = true in aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block.

name: S3 Versioning Disabled
description: S3 bucket versioning is disabled, preventing recovery from accidental deletion or overwrite and from ransomware attacks.
fix_example: Add aws_s3_bucket_versioning resource with status = Enabled. Consider also enabling MFA delete for extra protection against accidental or malicious deletions.

name: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
description: A deprecated or weak cryptographic algorithm (MD5, SHA-1, RC4, DES) is used for hashing, signing, or encryption.
fix_example: Replace MD5 with SHA-256 or SHA-3. Replace SHA-1 with SHA-256. Replace DES/3DES with AES-256-GCM. Replace RC4 with ChaCha20-Poly1305. Use TLS 1.2+ with strong cipher suites.
references: Rahman et al. 2019 (Seven Sins - Weak Cryptography)

name: Empty Password
description: A service or user account is configured with an empty password, providing no authentication barrier.
fix_example: Always set a non-empty password or disable password authentication in favour of key-based authentication. Use password: !!' in /etc/shadow to disable password login for service accounts.
references: Rahman et al. 2019 (Seven Sins - Empty Password)

name: HTTP Without TLS
description: A service is configured to listen on HTTP (port 80) without redirecting to HTTPS, transmitting data in cleartext.
fix_example: Configure HTTPS with a valid TLS certificate. Redirect HTTP to HTTPS at the load balancer or application level. In Kubernetes: configure TLS in Ingress resources. Disable HTTP listeners.
references: Rahman et al. 2019 (Seven Sins - HTTP without TLS)

name: Missing Network Policy
description: Kubernetes namespaces lack NetworkPolicy resources, allowing all pods to communicate freely without restriction.
fix_example: Define NetworkPolicy resources with explicit ingress and egress rules. Start with a deny-all default policy and then selectively allow required traffic with podSelector.

name: Missing Pod Security Context
description: Pod or container lacks a securityContext definition, relying on default (insecure) settings.
fix_example: Add securityContext to pod and container specs with: runAsNonRoot: true, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, seccompProfile.type: RuntimeDefault.

name: Insecure Default Namespace
description: Kubernetes resources are deployed in the default namespace rather than application-specific namespaces, reducing isolation.
fix_example: Create dedicated namespaces for each application or team. Apply RBAC, ResourceQuota, and LimitRange per namespace. Set namespace explicitly in metadata for all resources.

name: Missing Image Pull Policy
description: Container image pull policy is not set to Always for mutable tags, allowing stale or compromised images to be used.
fix_example: Set imagePullPolicy: Always when using mutable tags. Prefer pinned image digests (image@sha256:...) with imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent for immutable tags.

name: Missing Liveness and Readiness Probes
description: Container lacks liveness or readiness probes, preventing Kubernetes from detecting unhealthy pods and rerouting traffic.
fix_example: Add livenessProbe and readinessProbe to container spec. Use httpGet, exec, or tcpSocket probes with appropriate initialDelaySeconds, periodSeconds, and failureThreshold values.

name: Missing Kubernetes Secrets Encryption
description: Kubernetes Secrets are stored in etcd without encryption at rest, exposing them to anyone with etcd access.
fix_example: Enable EncryptionConfiguration for etcd. Use kms provider for envelope encryption with a cloud KMS key. Consider using Sealed Secrets or Vault for secret management.

name: Missing RBAC Least Privilege
description: RBAC ClusterRole or Role grants excessive permissions (e.g., wildcards, cluster-admin), violating the principle of least privilege.
fix_example: Replace wildcard verbs and resources with specific permissions. Use Role instead of ClusterRole where possible. Avoid binding to cluster-admin. Regularly audit RBAC permissions.

name: Missing Terraform State Encryption
description: Terraform remote state storage (S3, Azure Blob) is not encrypted, exposing sensitive state data including credentials.
fix_example: Enable encrypt = true in the S3 backend configuration. Use KMS key_arn for envelope encryption. Enable versioning and access logging on the state bucket.

name: Unencrypted ELB
description: An Elastic Load Balancer listener accepts HTTP traffic without HTTPS, transmitting data in cleartext.
fix_example: Configure HTTPS listeners on load balancers. Add SSL certificate. Redirect HTTP (port 80) to HTTPS (port 443) with a redirect action. Use TLS 1.2+ security policies.

name: CloudTrail Logging Disabled
description: AWS CloudTrail is not enabled or log file validation is disabled, preventing detection of unauthorized API activity.
fix_example: Enable CloudTrail in all regions with multi-region trail. Enable log file validation with enable_log_file_validation = true. Store logs in an S3 bucket with access logging enabled.

name: MFA Delete Disabled
description: MFA Delete is not enabled on S3 versioned buckets, allowing objects to be permanently deleted without multi-factor authentication.
fix_example: Enable MFA delete on S3 versioned buckets. This requires the bucket owner's MFA device for version deletion, protecting against accidental or malicious data destruction.

name: Public RDS Snapshot
description: RDS snapshot is set to public, allowing any AWS account to restore a copy of the database.
fix_example: Set the RDS snapshot sharing attribute to private. Restrict snapshot sharing to specific AWS account IDs using the aws_db_snapshot resource's shared_accounts parameter.

name: IAM Wildcard Policy
description: An IAM policy grants wildcard actions (Action: *) or resource access (Resource: *), violating the principle of least privilege.
fix_example: Replace Action: * with specific required actions. Replace Resource: * with specific resource ARNs. Use IAM Access Analyzer to identify unused permissions and generate least-privilege policies.

name: Security Group All Traffic
description: A security group rule allows all traffic (-1 protocol) or all ports (0-65535) from any source.
fix_example: Restrict security group rules to specific protocols, ports, and CIDR ranges. Separate inbound and outbound rules. Apply the principle of least privilege to network access.

name: VPC Flow Logs Disabled
description: VPC Flow Logs are not enabled, preventing visibility into network traffic for security incident detection and forensics.
fix_example: Enable VPC Flow Logs with aws_flow_log resource. Log both ACCEPT and REJECT traffic. Send logs to CloudWatch Logs or S3 for analysis. Set appropriate retention periods.

name: Lambda Without VPC
description: An AWS Lambda function is not configured within a VPC, potentially exposing it to internet access and preventing access to private resources.
fix_example: Configure VPC settings in the Lambda function resource with vpc_config block. Specify subnet_ids (private subnets) and security_group_ids. Ensure outbound internet access via NAT Gateway if needed.

name: KMS Key Without Rotation
description: An AWS KMS key does not have automatic key rotation enabled, increasing the risk from key compromise.
fix_example: Set enable_key_rotation = true on aws_kms_key resources. This enables automatic annual key material rotation while maintaining the same key ID, minimizing impact of key material compromise.

name: Missing S3 Access Logging
description: S3 bucket access logging is not enabled, preventing visibility into who accessed what objects.
fix_example: Add aws_s3_bucket_logging resource with target_bucket pointing to a logging bucket. Enable server access logging for all buckets, especially those containing sensitive data.

name: EKS Control Plane Logging Disabled
description: EKS cluster control plane logging is not enabled for all log types, limiting visibility into API server activity.
fix_example: Enable all EKS control plane log types: enabled_cluster_log_types = [api, audit, authenticator, controllerManager, scheduler] in aws_eks_cluster.

name: DynamoDB Without Encryption
description: DynamoDB table is not configured with server-side encryption at rest.
fix_example: Add server_side_encryption block with enabled = true to aws_dynamodb_table. Use AWS managed key (aws:dynamodb) or customer-managed KMS key for envelope encryption.

name: ElasticSearch Without Encryption
description: Amazon Elasticsearch/OpenSearch domain has encryption at rest or node-to-node encryption disabled.
fix_example: Set encrypt_at_rest.enabled = true and node_to_node_encryption.enabled = true in aws_elasticsearch_domain. Use a KMS key for server-side encryption.

name: SNS Topic Without Encryption
description: An SNS topic does not use server-side encryption (SSE), leaving message content unencrypted at rest.
fix_example: Set kms_master_key_id in aws_sns_topic resource to enable SSE. Use either the default aws/sns KMS key or a customer-managed KMS key.

name: SQS Queue Without Encryption
description: An SQS queue does not have server-side encryption enabled with KMS.
fix_example: Set kms_master_key_id in aws_sqs_queue resource. Use SQS SSE with AWS managed key (alias/aws/sqs) or a customer-managed KMS key.

name: Ansible Debug Module in Production
description: Ansible debug module is used to print variable values including sensitive data that may be visible in logs.
fix_example: Remove debug tasks from production playbooks. Use no_log: true on tasks that handle sensitive data. Configure Ansible to suppress sensitive variable output in logs.

name: Ansible Shell Module Without Restrictions
description: Ansible shell or command module is used with user-controlled input without input sanitization, creating command injection risk.
fix_example: Prefer Ansible modules over shell commands. When shell is necessary, validate and sanitize inputs. Use executable: /bin/bash -e to fail fast. Avoid passing user-controlled variables directly to shell.

name: Puppet Module Without Version Pin
description: A Puppet module dependency does not specify a version constraint, allowing unpredictable updates.
fix_example: Pin Puppet module versions in Puppetfile: mod 'puppetlabs/apache', '9.0.0'. Use semantic versioning constraints to allow patch updates: '>= 9.0.0 < 10.0.0'.

name: Chef Recipe Without Version Pin
description: A Chef cookbook dependency does not pin a version, creating supply chain risk from untested dependency updates.
fix_example: Pin cookbook versions in metadata.rb: depends 'apt', '>= 7.4.2', '< 8.0'. Pin in Berksfile.lock after resolving. Regularly audit and update pinned versions.

name: Missing Container User Namespace
description: Container does not use user namespace remapping, meaning container root maps to host root.
fix_example: Enable user namespace remapping in Docker daemon configuration. In Kubernetes, configure securityContext with runAsUser and runAsGroup to non-zero values.

name: Missing AppArmor or seccomp Profile
description: Container lacks AppArmor or seccomp security profiles, allowing unrestricted system call access.
fix_example: Add securityContext.seccompProfile.type: RuntimeDefault or Localhost to containers. Configure AppArmor profile annotations. Use Pod Security Standards enforce RuntimeDefault.

name: Terraform Hardcoded Region
description: AWS region or other provider-specific parameters are hardcoded in the provider block instead of being parameterized.
fix_example: Parameterize region with a variable: variable "aws_region" with a default value. Reference with region = var.aws_region. This enables environment-specific configurations.

name: Missing Ingress TLS
description: Kubernetes Ingress resource does not configure TLS, serving traffic over HTTP.
fix_example: Add tls section to Ingress spec with secretName referencing a TLS certificate secret. Annotate with cert-manager for automatic certificate provisioning.

name: ECR Image Scan Disabled
description: AWS ECR repository does not have image scanning on push enabled, missing vulnerability detection in container images.
fix_example: Set image_scanning_configuration.scan_on_push = true in aws_ecr_repository. Additionally configure Snyk or Trivy in CI/CD for image scanning before push.

name: Missing Deletion Protection
description: A stateful resource (RDS, DynamoDB, EKS) does not have deletion protection enabled, allowing accidental or unauthorized deletion.
fix_example: Set deletion_protection = true on aws_db_instance resources. Set prevent_destroy = true in lifecycle blocks for critical resources. Use AWS Organizations SCPs to prevent accidental deletion.

name: SSH Password Authentication Enabled
description: SSH is configured with PasswordAuthentication yes, allowing users to log in with just a password instead of requiring a cryptographic key, enabling brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks.
fix_example: Set PasswordAuthentication no in sshd_config. Use key-based authentication exclusively. Deploy SSH public keys with authorized_keys management. Enable ChallengeResponseAuthentication no as well.

name: Missing Multi-AZ Deployment
description: A database or service is deployed in a single availability zone, creating a single point of failure.
fix_example: Set multi_az = true on aws_db_instance resources. Deploy EKS nodes across at least three availability zones. Use aws_db_instance with multi_az for production databases.

name: SETUID/SETGID Binary in Container
description: A Dockerfile sets the SETUID or SETGID bit on a binary (chmod 4755 / chmod 2755). Any user who executes the binary gains the owner's privileges (typically root), enabling privilege escalation from within the container.
fix_example: Never use chmod 4755 or chmod u+s inside a container. Use capabilities (--cap-drop ALL) and rootless containers instead. If privilege is needed, use Linux capabilities at the container level via securityContext.capabilities in Kubernetes.

name: Remote Script Execution Without Integrity Check
description: A Dockerfile downloads and executes a remote script (e.g., curl URL | bash) without verifying its checksum or signature. A compromised or man-in-the-middle server can inject arbitrary code into the build.
fix_example: Download the script to a file first, verify its SHA256 checksum against a known-good value, then execute. Alternatively, COPY the script from a trusted source in the build context and verify. Avoid piping remote content directly to a shell interpreter.
references: CWE-494: Download of Code Without Integrity Check
OWASP A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures
